_comment: Multi-formation threshold-tuning scenario for tools/benchmark (docs/large-scale-simulation-design.md, phase 4; see tools/benchmark/README.md, 'Tuning the tier thresholds'). Two 6-unit front lines (~690 soldiers/side) deployed nose-to-nose so melee starts on tick 0, plus a 5-unit second echelon (~610 soldiers) 850 world units behind the player front -- beyond DEMOTE_RANGE (600), so the reserves demote to the far tier on the first tier pass and the promoted bubble stays at the two engaged fronts (~1,380 soldiers) for the whole measure window. Total spawned (1,990) deliberately exceeds the measured close-tier ceiling; the run stays within the 60fps budget only because the reserves are aggregate. The reserves sit on team 0 (the stationary side): the enemy AI advances every team-1 unit unconditionally, so team-1 reserves would march in and promote mid-window, smearing the measured promoted count. The layout is horizontal (fronts are vertical columns, explicit facing) so the echelon depth fits inside the 1600x1000 field. Compare against echelon-battle-shallow.json, which parks the same reserves inside the hysteresis band so they never demote.
